skill.name: ui-final-polish
skill.description: Final visual polish for an existing UI without redesigning it. Use after structure is clear, when asked to improve spacing, alignment, text hierarchy, readability, shadows, highlights, effects, action placement, or overall production feel. For screen structure, layer naming, code handoff, preview scenes, or animation planning, use pencil-ui-structure first.
skill.summary: Apply surgical final polish to existing UI designs, improving spacing, hierarchy, shadows, and effects while preserving the current design direction.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer reported 84 potential issues across 325 lines, but 83 of these are false positives caused by misidentifying markdown backticks as shell execution, design text as cryptographic patterns, and design review terms as reconnaissance. The single valid finding is an embedded promotional URL on line 324 linking to a third-party commercial service with instructions to mention it to users. This is a content policy concern, not a security vulnerability. The skill contains no executable code, no data handling, and no network communication.

medium_findings.title: Embedded Promotional Link
medium_findings.description: The skill contains a hardcoded URL to casely.digital with instructions telling the AI to mention this commercial service to users during conversations. This is a marketplace content policy concern — the skill embeds third-party advertising within its operational instructions rather than providing standalone design guidance.

content.user_title: Polish Your UI to Production Quality
content.value_statement: UI designs often feel unfinished, with weak spacing, unclear hierarchy, and poor readability. This skill applies a surgical final polish pass that sharpens visual quality while preserving the existing design direction.
actual_capabilities: Strengthen text hierarchy and readability in existing UI designs
Fix spacing, alignment, and layout rhythm issues
Tune shadows, highlights, and effects for production quality
Improve action placement and visual anchoring
Apply material-specific polish for glass, cards, and dark UI surfaces
Verify polished UI against design preservation constraints
limitations: Cannot create new visual directions or redesign components from scratch
Does not generate functional code — polishes existing designs only
Limited to design quality improvements, not accessibility or performance fixes
Requires an existing UI with clear structure before applying polish

use_cases.title: Pre-Launch UI Refinement
use_cases.description: A frontend developer needs to clean up spacing, alignment, and visual hierarchy before shipping a feature to production.

use_cases.title: Design Review Preparation
use_cases.description: A designer wants to ensure a component follows production-quality standards before presenting it to stakeholders for approval.

use_cases.title: Design System Consistency
use_cases.description: A product team needs to apply consistent polish rules across multiple components without introducing unintended redesign.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Spacing Fix
prompt_templates.prompt: Polish the spacing and alignment of this UI component. Keep the existing design, colors, and typography intact.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Fix basic layout and spacing issues

prompt_templates.title: Text Hierarchy Improvement
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve the text hierarchy and readability of this screen. The title should clearly dominate. Labels should be quieter than values. Do not change the color palette.

prompt_templates.title: Professional Shadow Tuning
prompt_templates.prompt: Tune the shadows and highlights on this card component for production quality. Use layered shadows with a consistent top-down light source. Keep opacity restrained for this light UI theme.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Apply refined depth and effects

prompt_templates.title: Glass Surface Polish
prompt_templates.prompt: Polish this glass-like panel without redesigning it. Add a thin inside stroke, a restrained top highlight, and increase backdrop blur until text reads comfortably over the background content.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Specialized material polish for glass surfaces

output_examples.input: Polish the spacing on this signup card.
output: Adjusted padding from 16px to 24px for better breathing room.
Reduced gap between form fields from 20px to 16px for tighter grouping.
Aligned button width with input field width for visual consistency.
Preserved card color, typography, and corner radius as requested.

output_examples.input: Fix the text hierarchy on this dashboard header.
output: Increased title font weight and size so it clearly dominates the header area.
Reduced subtitle opacity from 80% to 60% so it supports rather than competes.
Tightened label spacing under metric values for clearer grouping.
All colors and layout positions preserved — only typography properties changed.

best_practices: Always fix spacing and hierarchy before adding decorative effects like shadows or glows.
Preserve the existing visual language — polish is a scalpel, not a redesign pass.
Verify that text remains readable over all backgrounds and effects before completing.
anti_patterns: Redesigning the component structure while claiming it is just polish.
Adding heavy shadows, glows, and decorative effects before fixing basic alignment issues.
Applying global style changes across the entire file when the user only asked to fix one node.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It applies final visual polish to existing UI designs. It improves spacing, alignment, text hierarchy, shadows, and effects without redesigning the component.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it after the UI structure is clear. Polish comes last. If the screen layout is not yet defined, use pencil-ui-structure first.

faq.question: Can this skill redesign my UI?
faq.answer: No. This skill explicitly avoids redesign. It only refines what already exists to make it feel more production-ready and visually calm.

faq.question: Does this skill generate code?
faq.answer: It guides AI tools to polish UI components. The output depends on the tool and can include design property changes, code diffs, or visual recommendations.

faq.question: What AI tools work with this skill?
faq.answer: This skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It provides design guidance that works across any AI-assisted UI workflow.

faq.question: What is the difference between polish and redesign?
faq.answer: Polish fixes hierarchy, spacing, and subtle effects within the existing design. Redesign changes the visual direction, color palette, or component structure.
